title: Fix --pull=true||false and add --pull-never to bud and from (retry)
body:
(Replaces #1873 as it had lint issues that were timing out tests that I couldn't
track down easily)

Prior to this fix, if someone did `buildah bud --pull=false .` and the image in
the Containerfile's FROM statement was not local, the build would fail. The same
build on Docker will succeed. In Docker, when `--pull` is set to false, it only
pulls the image from the registry if there was not one locally. Buildah would never
pull the image and if the image was not locally available, it would throw an error.
In certain Kubernetes environments, this was especially troublesome.

To retain the old `--pull=false` functionality, I've created a new `--pull-never`
option that fails if an image is not locally available just like the old
`--pull=false` option used to do.

In addition, if there was a newer version of the image on the repository than
the one locally, the `--pull=true` option would not pull the image as it should
have, this corrects that.

Changes both the from and bud commands.

Addresses: #1675

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: Replaces #1873.  Same code as that one except I've changed the test to use busybox instead of alpine.  #1873 kept timing out with test and warning about lint issues in 3 files that it didn't name.  I've cut/pasted the changes here after rebasing from upstream in hopes that will cure the mystery lint issues and the tests will complete in the time required by the CI.
Still failing, I think this is a real bug, where pull is hanging perhaps?

I'm not seeing a difference in the pull times when running the tests by hand.  I'm still seeing that lint warning on 3 files that it's not naming.  Want to try and see if I can figure that out.  What's driving me daffy is I'd green buttons on this before I put a `/hold` on it.

I suspect the new code that we've added to inspect the image may be taking too long for travis ci.  Prior to this change, the ci was taking close to 45 minutes to complete.  With all the pulls that we do throughout the code, I think hitting up the inspect code was slowing it down past the 50 minute mark which we can't change (Travis limitation).  I've changed the code to do the inspect only if we have a --pull=true.  I'm not sure that will be enough as we have a lot of those scattered throughout all of the tests.
Can we just increase the travis timeout?
We can't increase the travis time out unless we pay for it.  The "free" Travis has an embedded 50 minute time out that you can't work around.
Well I am hoping to get off of travis when we finish migrating to Cirrus.
Green with 5 seconds to spare.  I've a thought for whittling the time down further.  Will implement in the morning.

I've just changed a little over a dozen of the tests.  The main change there was changing `--pull` to `--pull=false`.  This way if the image isn't already locally available (usually alpine or busybox), we go ahead and pull it.  With the new

OK, I've changed most but not all of the `--pull` options in the tests to `--pull=false`.  That way we won't hit up the repo to check the date.  If we have the image we'll do nothing, if we don't , we'll pull it.  

I also reworked the logic in new.go so that it now only checks the repo for the date of the image if the image is found locally.  Prior we always did that date check which really wasn't needed if we didn't have the image locally.  Between the two changes we should be much closer time wise for test completion than we were prior.

Happy green test buttons on this, and tests completing in just over 45 minutes on average.  FWIW, the changes since the last review was to new.go and how/when it does the repo lookup, that's been minimalized, then changing a lot but not all of the `--pull` options to `--pull=false` through  out the tests, especially the tests other than build or from.
Updated with the test nits picked.
Happy green test buttons.  Could use another LGTM, especially for the new.go change.  @QiWang19 @vrothberg @nalind any takers?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably ec970d5) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Sadly @TomSweeneyRedHat needs a rebase.
The PR that won't die!
repushed.   tests/from.bats and tests/run.bats had merg conflicts that I've corrected.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
